title: paronychia chartacea var. minima
intro: Paronychia chartacea var. minima, also known as dwarf paronychia, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Herbal preparations of Paronychia chartacea var. minima can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil, and have been used to create topical creams, salves, and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
